Days turned into weeks. Hunger gnawed at his ribs. He rationed fresh air like a precious currency. Some nights, the cold bit deep enough to stop his breath. He shivered under the thin plastic sheet, praying for the warmth of a shore. The horizon held secrets. Sometimes he saw a dark shape. Was it a bird or a rock? He stared until his vision blurred.

Then came the morning sun. Its golden fingers grazed the water. A glimmer caught his eye further out. He rubbed his eyes, but the sparkle remained. It was a vessel, small and distant, cutting through the foam. Elias scrambled onto the highest point of the debris. Arms raised, he pointed.

He waited for movement. Minutes passed like hours. Finally, engines hummed. Propellers churned the water. Boats sped toward him. People waved. Their shouts reached the top of his ears. Help had arrived. Tears stung his eyes, mixing with the sweat.

The crew pulled him aboard. Warm blankets covered his shivering form. Nurses checked his pulse. His voice finally returned. He spoke of the journey, of the struggle against the elements. They listened intently. Elias closed his eyes, resting in peace.<|im_end|>
